title: CCPA/CPRA — Data Broker Registry — Opt-Out Propagation Latency to Downstream Data Partners

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: HTTP Event Collector from consent platform and partner webhooks
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="consent:optout" earliest=-90d
| eval oo=strptime(optout_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left consumer_id [
    search index=privacy sourcetype="partner:ack" earliest=-90d
    | eval ak=strptime(ack_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | stats min(ak) as first_ack by consumer_id, partner_id
  ]
| eval lag_h=if(isnotnull(first_ack), round((first_ack-oo)/3600,2), null())
| where isnull(first_ack) OR lag_h>72
| stats count by partner_id
| sort - count
value: When consumers opt out of sale/share, partners must stop promptly. Measuring time from opt-out receipt to partner acknowledgment API or file manifest update demonstrates operational effectiveness beyond UI banners.
implementation: (1) Set `72` hours per contractual SLA; (2) include GPC batch jobs as `channel`; (3) legal escalation for chronic laggards; (4) exclude partners in wind-down via lookup; (5) evidence for CPRA “do not sell/share” audits.
visualization: Table (partners), Histogram (lag_h), Single value (consumers past SLA).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
